title: Hometest Terms of Use - Draft V1 January 2026
introduction: [Hometest] is operated by NHS England [and commissioned by the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC)].

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
paragraphs: 1.1. We (NHS England) have developed the [Hometest area of the NHS App **(Hometest App)**] so that you can access certain NHS home testing services through a mobile app on your phone or tablet. We have also made the same service available via the logged in area of the NHS website, which you can access using a web browser. These Terms of Use cover both channels.

Your use of the NHS website and the NHS App (outside the Hometest App) will continue to be covered by the NHS App and NHS Website Terms of Use, together with any relevant privacy policies and terms and conditions.
1.2. The Hometest App enables you to access the following services:

list: **"Testing Services"** the Hometest App enables the ordering and delivery of home testing services; these Testing Services are fulfilled by our partners; and
**"Hometest App Services"** the Hometest App allows you to access certain functionality within the App, such as availability checks, notifications about your test kit status and test results.

paragraphs: Further details are in section 6 below.
1.3. You may also be able to access health tests without using the Hometest App and nothing in these terms will affect such access.

1.4. Contacting us. If you have any queries about the Hometest App, you think there are any faults within the Hometest App (whether the mobile app or the logged in area of the NHS website) or you wish to contact us for any other reason, please visit our **[help and support page](https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-app/help/)**, where you can see FAQs and find out how to contact us.
1.5. How we will communicate with you.
If we need to contact you specifically we will do so by email, SMS or telephone call using the contact details you have provided.
See further details in section 6.

sections.heading: 2. When these terms apply
paragraphs: 2.1. Please read these Terms of Use, together with our [Hometest Privacy Policy](/home-test-privacy-policy) and Cookies Policy [Cookies Policy - coming soon] and any other separate terms that apply to the Testing Services (see below), before you use the Hometest App and Testing Services. By continuing to use the Hometest App, you agree to be bound by these terms.

2.2. Separate terms of use, privacy policies or cookies policies may apply to the Testing Services; further details will be made available to you via the Hometest App, at the appropriate stage of the ordering process.

2.3. The Hometest App is not a medical device and so is not regulated by the [Medicines and Health Regulatory Agency (MHRA)](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/report-a-non-compliant-medical-device-enforcement-process). If Testing Services or Hometest App Services are deemed to be medical devices, this will be made clear to you when you access the relevant service.

sections.heading: 3. Availability of Testing Services via the Hometest App
paragraphs: 3.1. It is free to register for and use the Hometest App.
3.2. In order to access the Testing Services via the Hometest App:

list: you must create and maintain an account on the NHS App [NHS App - coming soon]
you must reside in England
you must be aged 16 years or older.

paragraphs: If you are under 16, the Hometest App will signpost you to other services available to you.
3.3. Availability of Testing Services via the Hometest App may also be subject to local restrictions, such as availability of Testing Services in your geographic region.
[insert WORDING re reasonable usage – max number of tests per year etc]

sections.heading: 4. Accessing the Hometest App
paragraphs: 4.1. The Hometest App depends on you having access to the NHS App. You must keep your NHS App login account safe and secure, in accordance with the NHS App terms of use [NHS App Terms of Use - coming soon], to ensure ongoing access to the Hometest App.
4.2. You are responsible for making all arrangements necessary for you to access the Hometest App, including but not limited to:

list: a secure internet connection (see [Cyber Aware website](https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/cyberaware/home))
an appropriate device, operating system and browser that meet our minimum requirements found on our [help and support page](https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-app/help/)
if you want the mobile app, downloading it from either Google Play or Apple's App Store (subject to their terms and conditions)
using your own virus protection software (and regularly updating it)

paragraphs: 4.2. Your Hometest App is only intended to be used by you. If you permit any other person to use your account, you:

list: do so entirely at your own risk
must not share your user identification code or password with such persons
are responsible for their access and use of the Hometest App as if it were your access and use
must make sure such persons are aware of these terms and comply with them

paragraphs: 4.3. If you access or attempt to access the Hometest App from outside England:

list: we cannot guarantee you will be able to access the Hometest App or that it will function correctly
you are responsible for complying with any local laws that apply to you in the country from which you are using the NHS App

sections.heading: 5. Updates to the Hometest and NHS Apps
paragraphs: 5.1. From time to time we may automatically update the Hometest App or ask you to update it if you have the mobile app, to improve performance, enhance functionality, reflect changes to the operating system, or address security issues. This also applies to updates to the NHS App.
5.2. If updates are not installed you may not be able to use the Hometest App or you may find that its functionality and/or performance is impaired.

sections.heading: 6. Details about the Services
paragraphs: 6.1. The following services may be available to you through the Hometest App. Not all services and features are available to all users. This depends on factors such as where you live and the Testing Services available (as described above):

list: **"Testing Services"** provided by your Local Authority (LA) [and their third-party partners], who are responsible for fulfilling the tests you order, processing the tests and delivering the results to you
**"Hometest App Services"** provided via the Hometest App and operated by NHS England [and commissioned by the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC)]

table.caption: Name of home testing services with functionality and further information
rows:
Testing Services
(Hometest App allows you to access the service, but the service is provided outside the Hometest App)

Supply of home tests to you
Fulfilment of testing (once tests are returned)
Management of test results

[Hometest Privacy Policy](/home-test-privacy-policy)
Please see the terms and conditions and privacy policies operated by your Local Authority, for more details of how testing services work, terms applicable to your use and how these services use your data. These will be made available to you via the Hometest App

Enables access to Testing Services
View notifications about your test kit orders, testing status and results
Provides updates relating to the Hometest App and services available within it
Provides general health information and signposts to other services that may be available to you

sections.heading: 7. Using the services
paragraphs: 7.1. Home testing via the Hometest App is just one point of access to healthcare tests. You may also be able to access healthcare tests (without using the Hometest App) from other health services such as your GP.

7.2. Additional information, services and advice will be available from your doctor or other healthcare professionals. We recommend that you consider these additional services so that information and advice may be tailored to you and your needs.
7.3. In order for you to receive the intended benefits of the Hometest App and services accessed, you must ensure that all data provided by you is complete and accurate.
7.4. We do not hold or have access to your GP records, test results are not shared or transferred by NHS England to your GP or to any other NHS service.

7.5. If you are sharing information from the Hometest App with a healthcare professional, you should make them aware that the information (including any test results) may not show your complete medical records and is intended to support, not replace, the healthcare professional's knowledge, experience and judgement.
7.6. The Hometest App and services within it:

list: are not a substitute for seeking medical advice. Always follow any medical advice given by your healthcare professionals
are provided for information purposes including, to assist you to manage any conditions and treatments that you might require under the supervision and care of a healthcare professional
do not provide medical or clinical diagnostic services
are not intended to be prescriptive or authoritative in respect of a diagnosis, condition or treatment, and are not intended to provide information on which you should solely rely
reflect current knowledge and practice in NHS in England. However, medical knowledge and practice is constantly evolving and as such they may not reflect the latest knowledge or practice

paragraphs: 7.7. Please note your GP or other healthcare professionals remain ultimately responsible for your health and wellbeing (including but not limited to any diagnosis or other healthcare advice). If you have any questions about your medical records or other health-related information made available via the Hometest App, you should contact your GP or other healthcare professional directly, or call NHS 111 by dialling 111 from any landline or mobile phone free of charge.
7.8. We are not responsible for any delay or non-availability of home testing or for any delay or lack of response to any contact you make through the Hometest App.
7.9. The Hometest App may enable someone who cares for you to access Testing Services on your behalf. Access to such services must be authorised by you.
7.10. If you use the Hometest App on behalf of someone you care for, these terms and any other applicable terms and conditions still apply to your use and you:

list: must not share login credentials with anyone else, including the person you are accessing services on behalf of

are responsible for service access and use of the Hometest App as if it were your access and use, including being responsible for compliance with these terms and any other applicable terms and conditions in respect of your access

must, to the extent possible bearing in mind their age, condition and capacity, make the person you are accessing services on behalf of aware of: (i) your access and any steps you take on their behalf, and (ii) these terms and other applicable terms and conditions

paragraphs: 7.11. Where we have assessed it to be useful to Hometest App users, we may include links to national and local healthcare websites that are not integrated with the Hometest App or the NHS App. We do not monitor the content of such websites. Any link provided in the Hometest App is solely for your convenience. We do not accept any responsibility for any third-party website or third-party applications.

sections.heading: 8. Ending your use of the Hometest App
paragraphs: 8.1. You may stop using the Hometest App at any time. If you wish to cancel your account, you should contact us via the [help and support page](https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-app/help/).

8.2. If you breach any of these terms, we may prevent you from accessing the Hometest App and suspend Testing Services. We will notify you of this. If what you have done can be put right, we will give you a reasonable opportunity to do so.
8.3. If your NHS account is cancelled or your access to the Hometest App is suspended:

list: your access to the Hometest App will stop. You should delete the App from your devices
any personal data we hold about you will be dealt with in accordance with our data retention policy, which is set out in our Hometest Privacy Policy

all rights granted to you under these terms shall automatically cease without further notice (although your statutory rights - for example in respect of data protection as described in the privacy policy - are not affected)

sections.heading: 9. Your right to use the Hometest App
paragraphs: 9.1. We own or have the right to use all intellectual property rights used for the provision of the Hometest App, including rights in copyright, patents, database rights, trademarks and other intellectual property rights, ("NHS IPR").
9.2. You have permission to use the Hometest App for the sole purposes described in these terms. You need written permission from us or any other owner of NHS IPR to use these items in any other way.
9.3. Unless permitted by law or under these terms, you will:

list: not copy the Hometest App or any NHS IPR except where such copying is incidental to normal use
not rent, lease, sub-license, loan, translate, merge, adapt or modify the Hometest App or any NHS IPR
not combine or incorporate your (or any other) account in any other programmes or services
not disassemble, decompile, reverse-engineer or create derivative works based on the whole or any part of the Hometest App or other any NHS IPR
comply with all technology control or export laws that apply to the technology used by the Hometest App or other any NHS IPR

sections.heading: 10. Prohibited uses
paragraphs: 10.1. You may not use the Hometest App:

list: to collect any data or attempt to decipher any transmissions to or from our servers
in a way that could damage, disable, overburden, impair or compromise our systems or security
to transmit any material that is insulting or offensive
in a way that interferes with other users
in any unlawful manner or for any unlawful purpose
in a manner that is improper use or inconsistent with these terms
to act fraudulently or maliciously by hacking into the NHS App
to transmit, send or upload any data that contains viruses, Trojan horses, worms, spyware or any other harmful programs designed to adversely affect the operation of computer software or hardware
in connection with any kind of denial of service attack
unless it has been downloaded from official App Marketplaces (Google Play Store or Apple App Store) or you are accessing its services via the NHS website in a web browser

on any device or operating system that has been modified outside the mobile device or operating system vendor supported or warranted configurations. This includes devices that have been "jail-broken" or "rooted"

paragraphs: If you do any of the above acts you may also be committing a criminal offence, and we will report any such activity to the relevant law enforcement authorities. We will co-operate with those authorities by disclosing your identity to them.

sections.heading: 11. Our liability to you
paragraphs: 11.1. Although we make reasonable efforts to provide, maintain and update the NHS App it is provided "as is" and, to the extent permitted by law, we make no representations, warranties or guarantees, whether express or implied (including but not limited to the implied warranties of satisfactory quality and fitness for a particular purpose), that the NHS App or any particular services you access through the app (a) are accurate, complete or up-to-date; (b) will meet your particular requirements or needs; or (c) will always be available, error free, uninterrupted or free of viruses.

11.2. We are not responsible for Testing Services provided through the Hometest App. Testing Services, provided by third parties, will have an agreement with NHS England and undertake an integration process, but this does not mean that such services can be guaranteed or endorsed by NHS England.
11.3. We are not responsible for external links to or from the Hometest App and cannot guarantee these will always work or will be suitable for your individual needs.
11.4. Nothing in these terms excludes or limits our liability for:

list: death or personal injury arising from our negligence
fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation
any loss or damage to a device or digital content belonging to you, if you can show that a) this was caused by us and b) we failed to use reasonable skill and care to prevent this
any other liability that cannot be excluded or limited under English law

paragraphs: 11.5. Subject to clause 11.4 of these terms, we will not be liable or responsible to you or any person you use the Hometest App on behalf of for:

list: any harm, loss or damage suffered where this is not caused by i) our negligence (for instance, if there is a misdiagnosis of your condition or you make any independent decisions based on the information provided in the Hometest App we would not be liable); or ii) our breach of these terms
any loss or damage arising from an inability to access and/or use the Hometest App (or Testing Services) in whole or in part
any business loss (including but not limited to loss of profits, revenue, contracts, anticipated savings, data, goodwill or wasted expenditure)
any indirect or consequential losses that were not foreseeable to both you and us when you commenced using the Hometest App

paragraphs: 11.6. As Testing Services are also governed by separate terms of use, you should also read the limits of liability described in those terms of use.

11.7. This clause 11 does not affect any legal rights you may have as a consumer in relation to defective services or software. Advice about your legal rights is available from your local Citizen's Advice or Trading Standards Office.

sections.heading: 12. General
paragraphs: 12.1. These terms, and any other terms or policies referenced, set out the entire agreement between you and us in respect of your use of the Hometest App.
12.2. These terms do not give any rights to any third party to enforce any of these terms.

12.3. Each of the clauses and sub-clauses of these terms operates separately. If any part is determined to be invalid or unenforceable it will be superseded by a valid and enforceable provision that most closely matches the intent of the original and all other terms shall continue in effect.
12.4. Even if we delay in enforcing these terms we can still enforce them later.
12.5. The laws of England shall apply exclusively to these terms and all matters relating to use of the NHS App, and dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England.

sections.heading: 13. Changes to these Terms of Use
paragraphs: 13.1. We will keep these Terms of Use under review, and we may update them from time to time for example to reflect changes that we might make to our services or to reflect changes in the law or best practice.

13.2. Any changes we make to these Terms of Use in the future will be posted on this page; we will notify you by posting a new version of the document on this page and updating the date. We will also undertake to report on any significant changes affecting the Hometest App through a notification in the App.
These Terms of Use were last updated: [DATE] 2026
